segments.beat: overhead static shot, device face-up on neutral surface, display off, clear shell and PCB fully visible
slow orbital pan begins top-down, display activates showing lit game text on transparent pane, clear body and internal board remain visible throughout
continuous slow rotation around device long axis, display stays lit, shell transparency holds, D-pad and A/B buttons readable
camera angle shifts to three-quarter rear-high perspective, PCB traces and components visible through shell, display still on
orbit continues sweeping to side profile, device thickness evident, scale reads as pocket-sized against desk surface
camera moves to lower angle, display face more prominent, lit pixels on transparent pane clearly isolated against background
rotation slows, front-face near-level angle, button layout and clear resin shell surface quality most legible
camera lifts slightly to high three-quarter, full device silhouette, golden PCB traces visible through shell
final frames, angle holds near end-of-orbit, display remains on, device rests on desk — clip ends

demonstrates.motion_value: high — continuous orbital rotation keeps PCB and component visibility as persistent foreground fact for full clip duration
high — lit game pixels on transparent pane readable through most of orbit, especially 0:12-0:17 face-on window
low — scale implied by desk surface context but no hand in frame; size only inferrable
low — display shows game text/sprite content but no active gameplay motion visible; static lit screen only
low — resin surface catches light at low angle, finish visible, not a dedicated showcase pass

selection_note: Clean unbroken orbital on a lit device — transparency and see-through display both carry strong throughout; no hands or gameplay motion limits retro_gaming and pocket_scale; best pulled as a silent loop hero.

aesthetics.standout: clean desk background keeps it minimal
aesthetics.weakness: single static overhead angle entire clip - no coverage change, screen not readable
aesthetics.note: ~20s of one locked-off overhead with slight drift; device never at a flattering angle - reshoot with deliberate angles + light
